activities_projects.title: Quantitative Measurement of Respiration Rate
activities_projects.objective: Calculate the volume of oxygen consumed by germinating seeds using a respirometer
steps: Place germinating seeds in the flask and a small tube of KOH to absorb all evolved $CO_2$.
Seal the flask with a one-holed cork and insert one end of the U-tube.
Dip the other end of the U-tube into the colored water.
Record the rise in water level in the tube every 5 minutes for one hour.
Plot a graph of 'Rise in Water Level' vs 'Time' and calculate the rate of $O_2$ consumption.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Quantifies the metabolic rate of aerobic respiration and understands partial pressure changes in a closed system.
activities_projects.evaluation: Mathematical calculation of the rate of respiration based on the volume displacement in the tube (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Chromatography of Leaf Pigments
activities_projects.objective: Separate and identify different photosynthetic pigments beyond Chlorophyll-a
steps: Grind leaves in acetone to extract a concentrated pigment solution.
Apply a small drop of the extract on a line drawn 2cm from the bottom of the chromatography paper.
Suspend the paper in a jar containing a solvent (Ether/Acetone) so that only the tip touches the liquid.
Observe the separation into Chlorophyll-a (blue-green), Chlorophyll-b (yellow-green), Xanthophylls (yellow), and Carotenoids (orange).
Calculate the Retention Factor ($R_f$) for each pigment.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Analyzes the complex composition of the photosystem and the chemical properties of different pigments.
activities_projects.evaluation: Identification of at least four pigments and calculation of their $R_f$ values (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: In-depth Study of Nephron Filtration
activities_projects.objective: Model the selective reabsorption and filtration of the kidney
steps: Fill the dialysis bag with a mixture of starch and glucose (representing blood proteins and nutrients).
Place the bag in a beaker of pure water (representing the Bowman's capsule).
Test the water in the beaker after 30 minutes for starch (using Iodine) and glucose (using Benedict's).
Analyze why glucose passed through but starch did not, relating it to the size of pores in the glomerulus.
Research the role of ADH in the Distal Convoluted Tubule (DCT).
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Evaluates the molecular basis of ultrafiltration and the significance of size-selective membranes in excretion.
activities_projects.evaluation: Technical report on why proteins (modeled by starch) are not found in healthy urine (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Action of Pepsin on Protein
activities_projects.objective: Investigate the pH dependency of stomach enzymes
materials: Egg white (boiled and chopped into cubes)
steps: Label 3 tubes: A (Pepsin + Water), B (Pepsin + $HCl$), C (Pepsin + $NaOH$).
Add equal cubes of egg white to each and incubate in a water bath at body temperature for 2 hours.
Observe the degree of breakdown/turbidity change in each tube.
Discuss why Pepsin requires an acidic medium for activation from Pepsinogen.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Analyzes the biochemical environment required for protein digestion in the human stomach.
activities_projects.evaluation: Explanation of the optimum pH for Pepsin activity and the role of $HCl$ (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Hemolysis and Plasmolysis in Blood Cells
activities_projects.objective: Observe the effect of tonicity on animal cells (red blood cells)
materials: Fresh goat blood (from butcher) or prepared RBC slide
steps: Place a drop of blood on three slides.
Add a drop of isotonic, hypertonic, and hypotonic solution to respective slides.
Observe under high power: look for 'crenation' in hypertonic and 'bursting/ghost cells' in hypotonic.
Explain why plant cells do not burst in distilled water unlike animal cells.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Synthesizes knowledge of osmosis, cell wall function, and blood pressure regulation.
activities_projects.evaluation: Comparison of animal cell vs. plant cell behavior in different osmotic environments (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Double Circulation Flowchart & Pressure Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Analyze the blood pressure differences between Pulmonary and Systemic circuits
materials: Research data on Arterial vs. Venous pressure
steps: Draw a detailed 'Double Circulation' diagram including Pulmonary and Systemic loops.
Research why the left ventricle wall is thicker than the right ventricle.
Measure blood pressure in a volunteer and discuss the significance of Systolic and Diastolic readings.
Analyze why backflow is prevented in veins but not in arteries.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands the fluid dynamics of the heart and the efficiency of oxygen delivery in mammals.
activities_projects.evaluation: Diagram accuracy and explanation of ventricular hypertrophy in relation to pressure (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Effect of $CO_2$ Concentration on Photosynthesis
activities_projects.objective: Study the 'Law of Limiting Factors' in a controlled environment
steps: Set up the Hydrilla funnel experiment under constant light.
Vary the $CO_2$ concentration by adding 0.1g, 0.5g, and 1.0g of $NaHCO_3$ to the water.
Count the oxygen bubbles per minute for each concentration.
Determine the point where the rate levels off (Saturation point).
Explain the results using Blackman's Law of Limiting Factors.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Mastery of how environmental variables interact to determine the overall rate of life processes.
activities_projects.evaluation: Graph showing the effect of $CO_2$ concentration on the rate of bubble production (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Analysis of Vital Capacity
activities_projects.objective: Measure and compare the lung volume of different individuals
steps: Take a deep breath and exhale fully into a balloon.
Measure the diameter of the balloon or the volume of water it displaces.
Repeat for athletes and non-athletes (or smokers vs. non-smokers via research data).
Relate 'Vital Capacity' to the efficiency of cellular respiration and ATP production.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Links respiratory volume to overall physical health and metabolic efficiency.
activities_projects.evaluation: Calculation of approximate lung volume and comparative analysis (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Bile and Lipase: Synergistic Digestion
activities_projects.objective: Quantify the time taken for fat digestion with and without bile
steps: Add sodium carbonate to milk to make it alkaline (pink with phenolphthalein).
Tube A: Milk + Lipase. Tube B: Milk + Lipase + Bile salts.
Record the time taken for the pink color to disappear (due to fatty acid production lowering pH).
Calculate the rate of reaction for both tubes.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Provides kinetic evidence for the role of emulsification in enzymatic digestion.
activities_projects.evaluation: Rate calculation ($1/t$) comparison for both experimental conditions (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Transpiration Pull vs. Root Pressure
steps: Observe the rise of water in different sized capillary tubes.
Relate the 'surface tension' and 'cohesion' to the movement of water in tall trees.
Research the phenomenon of 'Guttation' and explain why it occurs only at night or in high humidity.
Diagram the 'Transpiration-Photosynthesis' compromise.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Integrates physical laws (capillarity, tension) with biological transport in xylem.
activities_projects.evaluation: Technical explanation of how water reaches the top of a 100-meter Sequoia tree (10 marks)
